title: leptosiphon ciliatus subsp. neglectus
intro: Leptosiphon ciliatus subsp. neglectus, also known as the San Francisco gilia or San Francisco lepidote, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the polemoniaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Leptosiphon ciliatus subsp. neglectus can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
